patch.value: docs-skills-gate: validate anchor fragments in relative links

2026-07-04 docs audit: scripts/release/docs-skills-gate.mjs splits links on [?#], so FILE.md#heading links only check file existence. All 12 current anchor links verified manually — nothing broken — but future dead anchors are ungated. Extend the gate to slugify headings in the target file and assert the fragment exists. Also break the wall-of-text paragraphs (EXTENSIONS.md line 89 ~4600 chars, line 114; COMMANDS.md lines 612, 627) into bullets while touching docs.
docs-skills-gate fails on relative links whose anchor fragment does not exist in the target document; gate green on current docs.

## Context
2026-07-04 docs audit: scripts/release/docs-skills-gate.mjs splits link targets on [?#], so FILE.md#heading links only verify the file exists — dead anchors are ungated. All 12 current anchor links were verified by hand (none broken); this closes the gap for future ones.

## Approach
In the gate, when a relative link carries a fragment: read the target file, slugify its headings (GitHub slugger rules — lowercase, strip punctuation, spaces→dashes, dedupe with -1 suffixes), and assert the fragment matches. While touching docs, break the wall-of-text paragraphs into bullets: EXTENSIONS.md line 89 (~4600 chars) and line 114; COMMANDS.md lines 612 and 627.

## Definition of done
Gate fails on a seeded dead anchor and passes on the current tree; the four wall-of-text paragraphs are restructured; gate runtime stays negligible in CI static gates.
